title: Refactor Codex capability reporting
description: Refactor Codex client capability modeling so init output can distinguish Codex official support from Fabric-installed state, and update the init summary / reason message behavior accordingly without changing hook installation yet.
test: Update existing init capability summary coverage so interactive Codex output no longer asserts generic manual-only wording; verify current config-install Codex TOML path still passes.
acceptance_criteria: Codex rows in init capability summary no longer imply Codex lacks skill/hook support; reason/follow-up messaging reflects partial-install state instead of unsupported/manual-only state; existing Codex MCP config behavior remains unchanged.
hints: Prefer additive status modeling over ad-hoc string special-casing || packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/config-install.test.ts
findings: Codex capability reporting now distinguishes supported vs installed for hook/skill, and init reason/follow-up messaging no longer presents Codex as unsupported. Added i18n states for supported/install-client-assets and updated the interactive Codex summary test. Verified with package-scoped vitest runs for init-mcp-scope and config-install.
acceptance_met: Capability table shows Codex support without implying installation; reason message now uses installable follow-up wording; Codex MCP config tests still pass

title: Add Codex repo-skill install path
description: Add a Codex-specific repo-scoped init skill installation path to `fab init` using a new template path that does not overwrite or depend on currently dirty Claude/Codex template files. Keep the write path non-destructive and scoped to new Codex assets only.
test: Add/extend init install tests to assert Codex init creates repo skill assets and preserves existing custom skill content on rerun/non-force flows.
acceptance_criteria: `fab init` installs a Codex repo skill under an official repo-discovery path; implementation avoids modifying currently dirty `templates/claude-skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md` and `templates/bootstrap/codex-AGENTS-header.md`; non-destructive behavior is covered by tests.
hints: Do not touch currently dirty template files; create new Codex-specific templates/assets and wire them from init only || packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-claude-install.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-nondestructive.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-force.test.ts;templates/claude-skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md;templates/bootstrap/codex-AGENTS-header.md
findings: Added a new Codex repo-skill install path under `.agents/skills/fabric-init/SKILL.md`, backed by new Codex-only templates in both root and packaged template directories. Extended init install, non-destructive, and force tests to cover the new path. This avoided editing the currently dirty Claude/Codex template files.
acceptance_met: Codex repo skill now installs on init; rerun without force preserves custom Codex skill; force overwrites Codex skill with template; no dirty template file was modified
